business.headline: Sensex falls 1,110 points, Nifty below 24,500; Tata Steel tumbles 7%The BSE Sensex fell 1,122.66 points, or 1.40%, to close at 79,116.19, while the Nifty 50 declined 385.20 points to settle at 24,480.50.

Tata Steel, L&T, JSW Steel: Why these stocks fell up to 8% todayShares of Tata Steel, Larsen & Toubro and JSW Steel plunged sharply as global tensions and rising crude oil prices rattled markets. Read on to understand what triggered the sell-off today.

Strait of Hormuz shutdown: Is India staring at an energy crisis?Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil route. Read on to understand what it could mean for India's energy supplies and economy.

Rupee slumps to all-time low against US dollar: Why is it falling?The rupee came under pressure as escalating tensions between the US, Israel and Iran triggered global risk aversion, while rising crude oil prices and a stronger dollar weighed on emerging market currencies.

L&T shares tumble over 7%: Why is the stock down today?L&T shares dropped nearly 7% during the session as investors turned cautious amid escalating tensions involving the United States, Israel and Iran.

Sensex down 1,500 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Dalal Street opened sharply lower on Wednesday as escalating geopolitical tensions and rising crude prices rattled global markets. Here are the key reasons behind today's sharp stock market fall.

Panic grips Dalal Street as war tensions sink Sensex by ovr 1,700 pointsDalal Street investors turned cautious as crude oil prices surged and fears grew that a prolonged war in West Asia could disrupt global energy supplies and stoke inflation.

Is the stock market open or closed on Holi, March 4?Wondering if you can trade stocks on Holi this year? While celebrations continue across India on March 4, here's what you need to know about NSE and BSE trading.

India has enough oil stocks, no immediate plan to hike fuel prices: Govt sourcesIndia has 25 days of crude oil and refined oil stocks and is scouting for alternative sources for importing crude oil, LPG and LNG, government sources have confirmed. There is no immediate plan to raise the price of petrol and diesel, according to government sources. However, there is bound to be impact where gas and oil is concerned with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. About 80 per cent of the oil that India depends on comes from Iran and through the Strait of Hormuz. The Strait of Hormuz has been shut for the past 48 hours. Energy analyst Chetan Bhutani states that 'strategic reserves can support LPG and LNG demand for about 15 to 20 hot days, if needed, while crude oil reserves are estimated to last about 40 to 45 days in the event of supply disturbances.'

23-year-old turns India's instant house help craze into $100 million startupFrom that single hub in Gurugram to a presence across 10 cities, the growth has been swift. Today, nearly half of Pronto's demand comes from the New Delhi region, with about a fifth from its home base of Bengaluru. The platform now clocks more than 18,000 bookings daily, and over 4,500 professionals worked at least one shift on the app last month.

Iran–US conflict puts India's basmati exports at risk, farmers fear lossesIndia sends nearly 72% of its total basmati exports to Middle Eastern countries. As conflict spreads in the region, uncertainty has started to cloud this important trade. If the war continues for a long time, exports could fall sharply, and farmers may end up paying the price.

India has enough oil stocks for 50 days, looking for more options: Govt sourcesGlobal oil prices rose for the third straight session on Tuesday. Traders are worried that supplies from the Middle East, one of the world's most important oil-producing regions, could be disrupted.

I have Rs 50,000. Should I open a shop or invest in financial assets?Rs 50,000 may not look like a big amount, but it can be the starting point of something meaningful. It could turn into a small business you build with your own hands, or quietly grow through smart investments. The choice you make today could shape your financial future.

Dubai caught in geopolitical storm. Will it hurt its billionaire-friendly image?Dubai has thrived by being the Gulf's safe address and has long promised stability in a turbulent region. Now, with conflict edging closer, that promise is being quietly tested.

India trims gas supply to industries after Qatar halts LNG production: ReportQatar halted its LNG production on Monday as Iran continued strikes in the Gulf in response to Israeli and US attacks. The situation has disrupted energy shipments and pushed up global fuel prices and shipping costs.

Gold jumps nearly Rs 5,000: Will prices continue to rise amid the US-Iran war?The yellow metal is shining once again as geopolitical uncertainty fuels fresh buying interest. As the US-Iran war unsettles global markets, investors are wondering, will gold continue its upward climb from here?

Why is stock market closed today when people are celebrating Holi on March 4?The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) have declared March 3 as the official trading holiday for Holi 2026 in their annual calendar.

EPFO interest stays at 8.25%: Why has the rate not increased this year?At a time when households are watching every rupee, even a small rise in EPF returns would have felt welcome. Instead, the rate remains at 8.25%. Here's why the retirement body has chosen stability over a hike.

Oil climbs for third day as US-Israel conflict with Iran threatens supplyThe main concern in the market remains the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway through which about 20% of the world's oil and gas supply passes.

Holi 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?The stock market is closed on March 3, on account of Holi 2026. While the exchanges have declared the holiday for Tuesday, the festival of colours will be observed across many parts of the country on Wednesday, March 4.

entertainment.headline: Is Cillian Murphy playing Voldemort in new Harry Potter series? Ananya Panday shares glimpse of Holi celebrations with her parents and pets Ananya Panday celebrated Holi with parents and pets. The actor shared a glimpse of the family festivities on social media.

Chikni Chameli is a hit but Shreya Ghoshal won't sing such a song again. Here's why Shreya Ghoshal addressed backlash over performing Chikni Chameli while choosing not to record similar songs today. general news.headline: US strike hit Iranian warship near Sri Lanka returning from India exerciseAn Iranian naval frigate sank off Sri Lanka after participating in naval exercises in India, triggering a large-scale rescue operation. Live: Iran fires ballistic missile at Turkey, Nato defence systems interceptUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict between Iran and the US-Israel alliance continues to intensify on Wednesday, the fifth day of direct military confrontation, with no immediate signs of de-escalation. At least 787 people have been killed in Iran, nearly 60 in Lebanon, around a dozen in Israel, and six US soldiers have also died. Israel launched fresh strikes on Iran and renewed attacks on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, including airstrikes in and around Beirut. Iran has retaliated with counterstrikes on Iran and US interests in the Gulf. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the escalating Middle East conflict.

JDU boss ready for Rajya Sabha nominationA significant political shift may soon unfold in Bihar as Chief Minister Nitish Kumar is set to be nominated to the Rajya Sabha, signalling potential changes in state leadership. sports.headline: South Africa vs New Zealand Live Score, semifinal: Kiwis look to break Proteas jinx South Africa (SA) vs New Zealand (NZ) Live Score, T20 World Cup: South Africa head into the semi-final at Eden Gardens on the back of a flawless campaign, having won all seven of their matches so far. MS Dhoni fined for overspeeding, traffic violation in RanchiMS Dhoni has been fined Rs 1000 after his vehicle was caught overspeeding in Ranchi. The e-challan was issued under Section 183 of the Motor Vehicles Act ahead of IPL 2026 preparations.

Semi-final 1 preview: South Africa hunt final spot in bittersweet Eden GardensT20 World Cup semi-final, SA vs NZ: South Africa have a bittersweet history with the Eden Gardens. Last year, they beat India in a Test match, which sent the team into a phase of renewed belief and transition. However, back in 2023, this is the same venue where they lost the ODI World Cup semi-final to Australia. Can the Proteas overcome New Zealand, who haven't been at their dominating best in the tournament?
